summary: Decompiled a large slice of the Data Delete menu module, with decomp.dev reporting a new 100% match for `mnDataDel_8024E940` and `.bss`, plus sizable partial improvements across the menu input/proc, setup, data, and animation paths. The PR also tightened `mndatadel.static.h` layout by turning adjacent scalar fields into `x3C[6]` and adding an explicit `MnDataDelGObjUserData` layout. `mnevent.c` and `mnsoundtest.c` changes appear to be formatting/include-order touchups only.

key_files.role: Primary decomp work; replaced stubs and improved Data Delete menu animation/input/setup functions.
Static layout/header changes for `MnDataDelData`, `MnDataDelGObjUserData`, model descriptors, and extern data.
Formatting/include-order touchups visible in the diff.
Formatting-only line wrapping touchups visible in the diff.

what_changed: Implemented `mnDataDel_8024E940`, which decomp.dev reported as a new 100% function match worth +300 bytes.

Added C for previously stubbed `mnDataDel_8024EA6C`, `fn_8024F318`, `fn_8024F840`, `fn_8024FD40`, `mnDataDel_8024FE4C`, and `mnDataDel_80250170`, all reported as partial matching improvements rather than complete matches.
Updated `fn_8024ECCC` to use a local `struct MnDataDelData* anim_data = &mnDataDel_803EF870` and access `anim_data->x30` instead of direct `mnDataDel_803EF8A0` references.
Modeled Data Delete menu object user data as `struct MnDataDelGObjUserData` with byte flags, an `HSD_Text* xC`, and `HSD_JObj* x10[8]`.
Changed consecutive `MnDataDelData` fields `x3C` through `x50` into `s32 x3C[6]`, enabling loop/indexed access for menu entries.
Added static descriptors `mnDataDel_804A0918` and `mnDataDel_804A0928` and extern data `mnDataDel_803EF8AC[2]` in `mndatadel.static.h`.
Added dependencies used by the new code, including `gobjuserdata`, `memory`, `gm_*`, `gmmain_lib`, `lbarchive`, `lbaudio_ax`, `lbcardgame`, `debug`, and `ty/toy` headers.
Implemented menu asset loading for `MenMainConDl_Top`, `MenMainCursorDl_Top`, and `MenMainWarCmn_Top` assets in `mnDataDel_80250170`.
Touched `mnevent.c` and `mnsoundtest.c` with line wrapping/include ordering; no functional behavior change is evident from the shown hunks.
smart_moves: Converting adjacent offset fields into `x3C[6]` let the code express cursor-indexed menu entry access and loops in a way that improved both readability and matching.

The explicit `MnDataDelGObjUserData` struct captured the real GObj user-data layout, including flag bytes, padding, text pointer, and cached joints, which was necessary for `.bss` and function matching progress.

Repeated animation toggles preserve the current frame with `mn_8022F298`, apply a temporary state via `HSD_JObjReqAnimAll` and `MOBJ_MASK`, animate, then restore with type mask `0x480`; this pattern is reusable for matching menu highlight/enable state code.

The code keeps menu proc transitions in the observed order: kill the active global proc with `HSD_GObjProc_8038FE24(HSD_GObj_804D7838)`, set up the next proc, then copy `HSD_GObj_804D783C` into `proc->flags_3`.
The implementation used exact-layout tactics such as `PAD_STACK`, byte-pointer flag indexing like `((u8*) &user_data->x3)[i]`, and local struct pointers to shape generated code.
decomp_lessons: For menu data tables, look for runs of scalar fields that are actually indexed arrays; refactoring them into arrays can unlock loops and improve matching.
When menu GObj code repeatedly accesses raw `user_data`, define an offset-preserving struct early, even if fields remain named `x0`, `x1`, etc.
Menu animation matching often depends on preserving and restoring JObj frame state around material/object mask animation updates.

Screen setup functions commonly load multiple `StaticModelDesc` asset groups, build a root JObj, attach cursor children, initialize user data, and then install a proc; keep that lifecycle order intact.

Automated match reports are strong evidence for what matched, but this PR had no human review discussion, so intent behind odd codegen choices should be treated as inferred from the diff rather than confirmed.
naming_conventions: Module symbols use the `mnDataDel_` prefix plus address suffix, such as `mnDataDel_8024E940`, `mnDataDel_803EF870`, and `mnDataDel_804D6C68`.
Unidentified internal callbacks/procs remain address-based `fn_8024....` names, including `fn_8024F318`, `fn_8024F840`, and `fn_8024FD40`.
Unknown struct fields preserve offset-style names such as `x0`, `x3C`, `x58`, and `x10`; the array `x3C[6]` preserves the base offset after replacing separate scalar fields.
Subsystem function prefixes remain intact: `HSD_*`, `gm_*`, `gmMainLib_*`, `lbAudioAx_*`, `lbArchive_*`, and `mn_*`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: `mnDataDel_8024E940_inline` wraps `mn_8022F298`; from the diff this appears to be a codegen-shaping inline used for the matched `mnDataDel_8024E940`.
Used `PAD_STACK(24)` and `PAD_STACK(16)` plus unused pad locals to control stack layout.
Used explicit casts to `struct mn_80231634_t*`, `HSD_JObj*`, `s32`, and `enum _HSD_TypeMask` to match call signatures and generated code.
Kept unusual comma expressions such as `(0, 1.0f)` and `(0, sp18)` in `mnDataDel_8024E940`, likely because they affected the exact output.
Repeated constants `0xFFU`, `MOBJ_MASK`, and `(enum _HSD_TypeMask) 0x480` around `mn_8022F3D8`/`HSD_JObjAnimAll` calls are part of the menu animation matching pattern.
Pointer iteration over `mnDataDel_803EF870.x3C` and byte indexing into `user_data` flags reproduced the original memory layout closely enough to improve multiple functions.
review_feedback: No human PR body, reviews, or review comments were present in the slice.
decomp.dev bot reported matched code at 66.41% (+0.01%, +300 bytes) and matched data at 38.72% (+0.00%, +48 bytes) for GALE01.
Bot reported 2 new matches: `main/melee/mn/mndatadel` `mnDataDel_8024E940` from 0.00% to 100.00%, and `.bss` from 33.33% to 100.00%.

Bot reported unmatched-item improvements for `fn_8024F318` to 88.08%, `mnDataDel_8024FE4C` to 92.99%, `fn_8024F840` to 80.21%, `.data` to 89.03%, `mnDataDel_8024EA6C` to 93.13%, `mnDataDel_80250170` to 75.62%, `fn_8024FD40` to 92.52%, `fn_8024ECCC` to 96.38%, and `.sdata2` to 68.52%.
follow_up_queries: Can the partially matched functions `fn_8024F318`, `fn_8024F840`, `fn_8024FD40`, `mnDataDel_8024FE4C`, and `mnDataDel_80250170` be pushed to 100% by factoring or reordering the repeated selection-animation code?
Should `MnDataDelGObjUserData` fields be semantically renamed once the cursor, confirmation state, disabled flags, text pointer, and cached joint meanings are verified?

Verify the relationship between `mnDataDel_803EF8A0` and `mnDataDel_803EF870.x30`; the diff changed use sites to `anim_data->x30` while the static symbol remains declared with a comment tying it to `mnDataDel_803EF870`.
Check whether future mndatadel-only PRs should avoid incidental formatting-only edits in `mnevent.c` and `mnsoundtest.c` to keep diffs focused.
